docs.title: ⚡ AioFast
docs.text: AioFast is an async-first Python framework that combines a Laravel/Django-style developer experience with the performance of Litestar (HTTP/API) and Aiogram (Telegram bots).

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• IoC container & service providers — automatic dependency injection.
  • aiocraft CLI — serve, key:generate, route:list, and make:* generators.
  • Controllers & routing — decorator-based controllers compiled to Litestar handlers.
  • Database — SQLAlchemy models, migrations, seeders, factories.
  • Auth, validation, logging, config — batteries included, fully typed.

Continue with the Quick Start.

docs.title: Core commands
docs.text: Command Description serve Boot the app and start the Litestar HTTP server key:generate Generate and write APP_KEY to .env route:list Print all registered routes about Show application & environment info

docs.title: Generators (make:*)
docs.text:
Command Creates make:controller <Name> HTTP controller in app/http/controllers/ make:model <Name> SQLAlchemy model in app/models/ make:command <Name> Console command in app/commands/ make:middleware <Name> HTTP middleware in app/http/middleware/ make:provider <Name> Service provider in app/providers/ make:service <Name> Service class in app/services/ make:bot-handler <Name> Aiogram handler in app/bot/handlers/

Tip

Options use the --key=value form, e.g. python aiocraft.py serve --port=8080.

docs.title: Run the HTTP server
docs.text:
python aiocraft.py serve --port=8000

Then open:

  • http://127.0.0.1:8000/ — example route
  • http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/health — example controller
  • http://127.0.0.1:8000/schema/swagger — OpenAPI docs

docs.title: Define routes
docs.text:
Routes live in routes/web.py and routes/api.py. Each module exposes register(routes, app):

from core.registry import RouteCollector


async def home() -> dict:
    return {"hello": "world"}


def register(routes: RouteCollector, app) -> None:
    routes.get("/", home, name="home")
